name: Wireless Display
subtitle: iPhone/iPad as a wireless Mac display

apps.name: Wireless Display
apps.subtitle: Wireless extended display for your Mac
apps.localizedDescription: Turn your iPhone or iPad into a wireless extended display for your Mac over Wi-Fi. Run the macOS sender, then connect from here. Alpha.
apps.versionDescription: Major smoothness/latency overhaul: instant recovery from lost frames (no more multi-second corruption/freezes), reorder-tolerant receiving, lower-latency decode path, keyframe protection.

versions.localizedDescription: Major smoothness/latency overhaul: instant recovery from lost frames (no more multi-second corruption/freezes), reorder-tolerant receiving, lower-latency decode path, keyframe protection.
Much lower latency: removed an internal video buffer and switched to immediate decode. Plus the loss-recovery from 1.3 for fewer dropped frames.
New app icon. Packet-loss redundancy for smoother playback on lossy/isolated Wi-Fi. Disconnect screen dimming + 3-finger HUD toggle.
Big smoothness fix: packet-loss redundancy so frames/keyframes survive lossy Wi-Fi; the receiver no longer re-shows frames. Plus disconnect screen dimming and 3-finger HUD toggle.
Disconnect screen is now readable (dims the frozen frame); 3-finger tap shows/hides the HUD; works on Wi-Fi with client isolation (peer-to-peer).
